---
title: "Creating microposts"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: true
---

So I'm on the 4th day of my micropost publishing routine. Today I reactivated my thunder compute account 
and pulled my git repo to create my post from the thunder instance. The point is I'm going to need GPUs for
my image analysis content and business. So Thunder compute is a service platform where you can rent GPUs and a remote 
server location for your compute needs. It's great and affordable but it doesn't simpultaneously provide storage. 
To solve the storage problem, one hooks up another storage service such as a google account or aws. Then one pulls 
data on demand during trainin or inference. It seems clunky but it scales. 

# A Test Plot

```{python}
%matplotlib inline
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

plt.plot([1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6])
plt.title("Test Chart")
```
